data.title: Table No. 86: Details of Potential Disasters within the Sub-Metropolitan City
rows: Rivers and streams cause erosion and inundation of land and settlements during monsoon. Numerous families are affected annually.
Blowing away roofs of houses made of thatch, straw, or tile; problems with lightning strikes.
Increase in waterborne diseases like diarrhea, lack of resources and awareness, lack of adaptive capacity.
Electrical short circuits, human negligence, dense settlements, lack of fire-fighting equipment, other causes.
Impacts cereal crops and roofs of unpaved houses.
Lack of pure drinking water and sanitation, unmanaged poultry farming, swine flu, etc.
Nepal ranks 11th in seismic risk; building code not fully implemented in municipal buildings.
Vulnerable groups like elderly and children fall ill; extreme poor families highly affected.
Extreme heat reaching up to 46° causing fainting, swelling, cramping, and mental health issues.
Impacts the production of food crops and vegetable crops due to drought.
Damage to crops and production due to insects along with climate change.
